2603.16576.title: REFORGE: Multi-modal Attacks Reveal Vulnerable Concept Unlearning in Image Generation Models
2603.16576.paper_summary: Recent progress in image generation models (IGMs) enables high-fidelity content creation but also amplifies risks, including the reproduction of copyrighted content and the generation of offensive content. Image Generation Model Unlearning (IGMU) mitigates these risks by removing harmful concepts without full retraining. Despite growing attention, the robustness under adversarial inputs, particularly image-side threats in black-box settings, remains underexplored. To bridge this gap, we present REFORGE, a black-box red-teaming framework that evaluates IGMU robustness via adversarial image prompts. REFORGE initializes stroke-based images and optimizes perturbations with a cross-attention-guided masking strategy that allocates noise to concept-relevant regions, balancing attack efficacy and visual fidelity. Extensive experiments across representative unlearning tasks and defenses demonstrate that REFORGE significantly improves attack success rate while achieving stronger semantic alignment and higher efficiency than involved baselines. These results expose persistent vulnerabilities in current IGMU methods and highlight the need for robustness-aware unlearning against multi-modal adversarial attacks. Our code is at: https://github.com/Imfatnoily/REFORGE.
2603.16576.paper_summary_zh: 最近在影像生成模型（IGMs）方面的進展使得高保真內容創作成為可能，但也放大了風險，包括重製受版權保護的內容和生成冒犯性內容。影像生成模型去學習（IGMU）通過去除有害概念來減輕這些風險，而無需完全重新訓練。儘管受到越來越多的關注，但在對抗性輸入下的穩健性，特別是在黑箱環境中的影像側威脅，仍然未被充分探索。為了填補這一空白，我們提出了REFORGE，一個黑箱紅隊框架，通過對抗性影像提示來評估IGMU的穩健性。REFORGE初始化基於筆劃的影像，並利用交叉注意力引導的遮罩策略來優化擾動，將噪聲分配到與概念相關的區域，平衡攻擊效果和視覺保真度。在代表性的去學習任務和防禦措施中進行的廣泛實驗表明，REFORGE顯著提高了攻擊成功率，同時實現了比相關基準更強的語義對齊和更高的效率。這些結果揭示了當前IGMU方法中持續存在的脆弱性，並強調了針對多模態對抗性攻擊的穩健性意識去學習的必要性。我們的代碼位於：https://github.com/Imfatnoily/REFORGE。
2603.16576.author: Yong Zou et.al.
2603.16576.authors: Yong Zou, Haoran Li, Fanxiao Li, Shenyang Wei, Yunyun Dong, Li Tang, Wei Zhou, Renyang Liu
